title: Objects, Classes, Namespaces, and Binding · Independent Program Trial
summary: Model a library circulation domain while tracing real Python object and attribute behavior. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an inspectable library domain model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about object identity, type, value, mutability, equality, and lifetime in an object-oriented design.
Define classes and create instances while distinguishing class objects from the instances they construct.
Trace class, instance, module, and local namespaces during attribute lookup and assignment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
